title: Case labels with different width than case expression don't match
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

test.v:
```
module top(output reg a, b, c);
	always @* begin
		a = 0;
		b = 0;
		case (2'b0)
			1'b0:    a = 1;
			default: b = 1;
		endcase
		c = 1'b0 == 2'b0;
	end
endmodule

```
Run:
`yosys -p "synth_ice40 -abc2 -blif test.blif" test.v`

## Expected behavior

I expect that a==1, b==0 and c==1.

## Actual behavior

In the case statement equality seems to be determined differently. 2'b0 is not equal to 1'b0 in the case statement. But they are equal for the equality operator (==).
So the result I get is a==0, b==1 and c==1.

It seems to behave correctly if the case expression is not a literal or constant, like this:
```
wire [1:0] test_wire = 0;
...
case (test_wire)
	1'b0:  ...
	default:  ...
endcase

```
In this case the label 1'b0 will be chosen, even though its width doesn't match the two bit width of the wire.
